ChatError::ToolNotFound(call.name.clone()))?;\n197\t\n198\t        // Check if confirmation is required\n199\t        if tool.requires_confirmation()\n200\t            && let Some(confirmation) = tool.get_confirmation(&call.parameters, context).await\n201\t        {\n202\t            return Err(ChatError::ConfirmationRequired(serde_json::to_string(\n203\t                &confirmation,\n204\t            )?));\n205\t        }\n206\t\n207\t        tool.execute(call.parameters.clone(), context).await\n208\t    }\n209\t\n210\t    /// Create a registry with all built-in tools.\n211\t    pub fn with_builtin_tools() -> Self {\n212\t        let mut registry = Self::new();\n213\t\n214\t        // Read-only tools\n215\t        registry.register(GetWorkerStatusTool);\n216\t        registry.register(GetTaskQueueTool);\n217\t        registry.register(GetCostAnalyticsTool);\n218\t        registry.register(GetSubscriptionUsageTool);\n219\t        registry.register(GetActivityLogTool);\n220\t\n221\t        // Action tools\n222\t        registry.register(SpawnWorkerTool);\n223\t        registry.register(KillWorkerTool);\n224\t        registry.register(AssignTaskTool);\n225\t        registry.register(PauseWorkersTool);\n226\t        registry.register(ResumeWorkersTool);\n227\t\n228\t        registry\n229\t    }\n230\t}\n231\t\n232\timpl Default for ToolRegistry {\n233\t    fn default() -> Self {\n234\t        Self::with_builtin_tools()\n235\t    }\n236\t}\n237\t\n238\t/// Tool definition for the API.\n239\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n240\tpub struct ToolDefinition {\n241\t    /// Tool name.\n242\t    pub name: String,\n243\t\n244\t    /// Tool description.\n245\t    pub description: String,\n246\t\n247\t    /// Input schema (JSON Schema).\n248\t    pub input_schema: serde_json::Value,\n249\t}\n250\t\n251\t// ============ Built-in Read-Only Tools ============\n252\t\n253\t/// Get worker status tool.\n254\tpub struct GetWorkerStatusTool;\n255\t\n256\t#[async_trait]\n257\timpl ChatTool for GetWorkerStatusTool {\n258\t    fn name(&self) -> &str {\n259\t        \"get_worker_status\"\n260\t    }\n261\t\n262\t    fn description(&self) -> &str {\n263\t        \"Get the current status of all workers in the pool, including their health, activity, and assigned tasks.\"\n264\t    }\n265\t\n266\t    fn parameters_schema(&self) -> serde_json::Value {\n267\t        serde_json::json!({\n268\t            \"type\": \"object\",\n269\t            \"properties\": {\n270\t                \"worker_id\": {\n271\t                    \"type\": \"string\",\n272\t                    \"description\": \"Optional: Filter by specific worker ID\"\n273\t                },\n274\t                \"status_filter\": {\n275\t                    \"type\": \"string\",\n276\t                    \"enum\": [\"all\", \"healthy\", \"idle\", \"unhealthy\"],\n277\t                    \"description\": \"Filter workers by status\"\n278\t                }\n279\t            }\n280\t        })\n281\t    }\n282\t\n283\t    async fn execute(\n284\t        &self,\n285\t        params: serde_json::Value,\n286\t        context: &DashboardContext,\n287\t    ) -> Result<ToolResult> {\n288\t        let worker_id = params.get(\"worker_id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str());\n289\t        let status_filter = params\n290\t            .get(\"status_filter\")\n291\t            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n292\t            .unwrap_or(\"all\");\n293\t\n294\t        let workers = if let Some(id) = worker_id {\n295\t            context\n296\t                .workers\n297\t                .iter()\n298\t                .filter(|w| w.session_name == id)\n299\t                .cloned()\n300\t                .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n301\t        } else {\n302\t            context.workers.clone()\n303\t        };\n304\t\n305\t        let filtered: Vec<_> = workers\n306\t            .into_iter()\n307\t            .filter(|w| match status_filter {\n308\t                \"healthy\" => w.is_healthy,\n309\t                \"idle\" => w.is_idle,\n310\t                \"unhealthy\" => !w.is_healthy,\n311\t                _ => true,\n312\t            })\n313\t            .collect();\n314\t\n315\t        let total = context.workers.len();\n316\t        let healthy = context.workers.iter().filter(|w| w.is_healthy).count();\n317\t        let idle = context.workers.iter().filter(|w| w.is_idle).count();\n318\t\n319\t        Ok(ToolResult::success(\n320\t            serde_json::json!({\n321\t                \"total\": total,\n322\t                \"healthy\": healthy,\n323\t                \"idle\": idle,\n324\t                \"unhealthy\": total - healthy,\n325\t                \"workers\": filtered\n326\t            }),\n327\t            format!(\n328\t                \"Found {} workers ({} healthy, {} idle)\",\n329\t                total, healthy, idle\n330\t            ),\n331\t        ))\n332\t    }\n333\t}\n334\t\n335\t/// Get task queue tool.\n336\tpub struct GetTaskQueueTool;\n337\t\n338\t#[async_trait]\n339\timpl ChatTool for GetTaskQueueTool {\n340\t    fn name(&self) -> &str {\n341\t        \"get_task_queue\"\n342\t    }\n343\t\n344\t    fn description(&self) -> &str {\n345\t        \"Get the current task queue with ready beads, including priority and assignment information.\"\n346\t    }\n347\t\n348\t    fn parameters_schema(&self) -> serde_json::Value {\n349\t        serde_json::json!({\n350\t            \"type\": \"object\",\n351\t            \"properties\": {\n352\t                \"workspace\": {\n353\t                    \"type\": \"string\",\n354\t                    \"description\": \"Optional: Filter by workspace path\"\n355\t                },\n356\t                \"priority\": {\n357\t                    \"type\": \"string\",\n358\t                    \"enum\": [\"P0\", \"P1\", \"P2\", \"P3\", \"P4\"],\n359\t                    \"description\": \"Optional: Filter by priority\"\n360\t                },\n361\t                \"limit\": {\n362\t                    \"type\": \"integer\",\n363\t                    \"description\": \"Maximum number of tasks to return (default: 20)\"\n364\t                }\n365\t            }\n366\t        })\n367\t    }\n368\t\n369\t    async fn execute(\n370\t        &self,\n371\t        params: serde_json::Value,\n372\t        context: &DashboardContext,\n373\t    ) -> Result<ToolResult> {\n374\t        let workspace = params.get(\"workspace\").and_then(|v| v.as_str());\n375\t        let priority = params.get(\"priority\").and_then(|v| v.as_str());\n376\t        let limit = params.get(\"limit\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()).unwrap_or(20) as usize;\n377\t\n378\t        let mut tasks = context.tasks.clone();\n379\t\n380\t        if let Some(ws) = workspace {\n381\t            tasks.retain(|t| t.workspace.contains(ws));\n382\t        }\n383\t\n384\t        if let Some(p) = priority {\n385\t            tasks.retain(|t| t.priority == p);\n386\t        }\n387\t\n388\t        let total = tasks.len();\n389\t        tasks.truncate(limit);\n390\t\n391\t        let in_progress = context.tasks.iter().filter(|t| t.in_progress).count();\n392\t\n393\t        Ok(ToolResult::success(\n394\t            serde_json::json!({\n395\t                \"total_ready\": total,\n396\t                \"in_progress\": in_progress,\n397\t                \"beads\": tasks\n398\t            }),\n399\t            format!(\"{} ready tasks, {} in progress\", total, in_progress),\n400\t        ))\n401\t    }\n402\t}\n403\t\n404\t/// Get cost analytics tool.\n405\tpub struct GetCostAnalyticsTool;\n406\t\n407\t#[async_trait]\n408\timpl ChatTool for GetCostAnalyticsTool {\n409\t    fn name(&self) -> &str {\n410\t        \"get_cost_analytics\"\n411\t    }\n412\t\n413\t    fn   .unwrap_or(\"today\");\n439\t\n440\t        // Return the cost analytics from context\n441\t        let costs = match timeframe {\n442\t            \"today\" => &context.costs_today,\n443\t }\n494\t\n495\t    fn description(&self) -> &str {\n496\t        \"Get recent activity log entries including worker events, task completions, and errors.\"\n497\t    }\n498\t\n499\t    fn                   \"type\": \"integer\",\n505\t                    \"description\": \"Number of hours of history to retrieve (default: 1)\"\n506\t                },\n507\t                \"filter\": .get(\"filter\")\n524\t            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n525\t            .unwrap_or(\"all\");\n526\t\n527\t        let mut events = context.recent_events.clone();\n528\t\n529\t        if filter ============\n543\t\n544\t/// Spawn worker tool.\n545\tpub struct SpawnWorkerTool;\n546\t\n547\t#[async_trait]\n548\timpl ChatTool for SpawnWorkerTool {\n549\t    fn name(&self) -> &str {\n550\t        \"spawn_worker\"\n551\t    }\n552\t\n553\t    fn description(&self) -> &str {\n554\t        \"Spawn one or more new workers with a specified model type.\"\n555\t    }\n556\t\n557\t    fn parameters_schema(&self) -> serde_json::Value {\n558\t        serde_json::json!({\n559\t            \"type\": \"object\",\n560\t            \"properties\": {\n561\t                \"worker_type\": {\n562\t                    \"type\": \"string\",\n563\t                    \"enum\": [\"sonnet\", \"opus\", \"haiku\", \"glm\"],\n564\t                    \"description\": \"Type of worker to spawn\"\n565\t                },\n566\t                \"count\": {\n567\t                    \"type\": \"integer\",\n568\t                    \"description\": \"Number of workers to spawn (default: 1)\"\n569\t                },\n570\t                \"workspace\": {\n571\t                    \"type\": \"string\",\n572\t                    \"description\": \"Optional: Workspace to assign workers to\"\n573\t                }\n574\t            },\n575\t            \"required\": [\"worker_type\"]\n576\t        })\n577\t    }\n578\t\n579\t    fn requires_confirmation(&self) -> bool {\n580\t        true\n581\t    }\n582\t\n583\t    async fn get_confirmation(\n584\t        &self,\n585\t        params: &serde_json::Value,\n586\t        _context: &DashboardContext,\n587\t    ) -> Option<ActionConfirmation> {\n588\t        let count = params.get(\"count\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()).unwrap_or(1);\n589\t        let worker_type = params\n590\t            .get(\"worker_type\")\n591\t            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n592\t            .unwrap_or(\"unknown\");\n593\t\n594\t        // Auto-confirm if spawning 2 or fewer workers\n595\t        if count <= 2 {\n596\t            return None;\n597\t        }\n598\t\n599\t        Some(ActionConfirmation {\n600\t            title: format!(\"Spawn {} {} workers?\", count, worker_type),\n601\t            description: format!(\n602\t                \"This will spawn {} new {} workers. This may increase costs.\",\n603\t                count, worker_type\n604\t            ),\n605\t            level: ConfirmationLevel::Warning,\n606\t            cost_impact: None,\n607\t            affected_items: vec![format!(\"{} new workers\", count)],\n608\t            reversible: true,\n609\t        })\n610\t    }\n611\t\n612\t    async fn execute(\n613\t        &self,\n614\t        params: serde_json::Value,\n615\t        context: &DashboardContext,\n616\t    ) -> Result<ToolResult> {\n617\t        let worker_type = params\n618\t            .get(\"worker_type\")\n619\t            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n620\t            .ok_or_else(|| ChatError::ToolExecutionFailed(\"worker_type is required\".to_string()))?;\n621\t\n622\t        let count = params.get(\"count\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()).unwrap_or(1) as usize;\n623\t        let workspace = params.get(\"workspace\").and_then(|v| v.as_str());\n624\t\n625\t        // Check if worker spawner is available\n626\t        let spawner = context.worker_spawner.as_ref()\n627\t            .ok_or_else(|| ChatError::ToolExecutionFailed(\n628\t                \"Worker spawning not available. Please use the FORGE TUI to spawn workers.\".to_string()\n629\t            ))?;\n630\t\n631\t        // Spawn workers using the real spawner\n632\t        let workspace_path = workspace.map(std::path::PathBuf::from);\n633\t        match spawner.spawn_workers(worker_type, count, workspace_path.as_ref()).await {\n634\t            Ok(spawned) => {\n635\t                let mut result = ToolResult::success(\n636\t                    serde_json::json!({\n637\t                        \"spawned\": spawned,\n638\t                        \"count\": spawned.len(),\n639\t                        \"worker_type\": worker_type,\n640\t                        \"workspace\": workspace\n641\t                    }),\n642\t                    format!(\"Spawned {} {} worker(s)\", spawned.len(), worker_type),\n643\t                );\n644\t\n645\t                for s in &spawned {\n646\t                    result = result.with_side_effect(SideEffect {\n647\t                        effect_type: \"spawn\".to_string(),\n648\t                        description: format!(\"Spawned worker {} (PID: {})\", s.session_name, s.pid),\n649\t                        data: Some(serde_json::json!({\n650\t                            \"session\": s.session_name,\n651\t                            \"worker_id\": s.worker_id,\n652\t                            \"pid\": s.pid\n653\t                        })),\n654\t                    });\n655\t                }\n656\t\n657\t                Ok(result)\n658\t            }\n659\t            Err(e) => Ok(ToolResult::error(format!(\"Failed to spawn workers: {}\", e)))\n660\t        }\n661\t    }\n662\t}\n663\t\n664\t/// Kill worker tool.\n665\tpub struct KillWorkerTool;\n666\t\n667\t#[async_trait]\n668\timpl ChatTool for KillWorkerTool {\n669\t    fn name(&self) -> &str {\n670\t        \"kill_worker\"\n671\t    }\n672\t\n673\t    fn description(&self)          \"type\": \"object\",\n680\t            \"properties\": {\n681\t                \"session_name\": {\n682\t                    \"type\": \"string\",\n683\t                    \"description\": requires_confirmation(&self) -> bool {\n691\t        true\n692\t    }\n693\t\n694\t    async fn get_confirmation(\n695\t        &self,\n696\t        params: &serde_json::Value,\n697\t        context: &DashboardContext,\n698\t    ) -> Option<ActionConfirmation> {\n699\t        let session_name = params.get(\"session_name\").and_then(|v| v.as_str())?;\n700\t\n701\t        // Find the worker\n702\t        let worker = context\n703\t            .workers\n704\t            .iter()\n705\t            .find(|w| w.session_name == session_name)?;\n706\t\n707\t        Some(ActionConfirmation {\n708\t            title: format!(\"Kill worker {}?\", session_name),\n709\t            description: if worker.current_task.is_some() {\n710\t                format!(\n711\t                    \"Worker {} is currently working on a task. ConfirmationLevel::Warning\n721\t            },\n722\t            cost_impact: None,\n723\t            affected_items: vec![session_name.to_string()],\n724\t            reversible: true,\n725\t        })\n726\t    }\n727\t\n728\t    async fn execute(\n729\t        &self,\n730\t        params: serde_json::Value,\n731\t        _context: &DashboardContext,\n732\t    ) -> Result<ToolResult> {\n733\t        let session_name = params\n734\t            .get(\"session_name\")\n735\t            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n736\t            .ok_or_else(|| {\n737\t                ChatError::ToolExecutionFailed(\"session_name is required\".to_string())\n738\t            })?;\n739\t\n740\t        // In a real implementation, this would call tmux to kill the session\n741\t        Ok(ToolResult::success(\n742\t            serde_json::json!({\n743\t                \"killed\": session_name\n744\t            }),\n745\t            format!(\"Killed worker {}\", session_name),\n746\t        )\n747\t        .with_side_effect(SideEffect {\n748\t            effect_type: \"kill\".to_string(),\n749\t            description: format!(\"Killed worker {}\", session_name),\n750\t            data: Some(serde_json::json!({\"session\": session_name})),\n751\t        }))\n752\t    }\n753\t}\n754\t\n755\t/// Assign task tool.\n756\tpub struct AssignTaskTool;\n757\t\n758\t#[async_trait]\n759\timpl ChatTool for AssignTaskTool {\n760\t    fn name(&self) -> &str {\n761\t        \"assign_task\"\n762\t    }\n763\t\n764\t    fn description(&self) -> &str {\n765\t        \"Assign or reassign a task/bead to a specific model tier.\"\n766\t    }\n767\t\n768\t    fn parameters_schema(&self) -> serde_json::Value {\n769\t        serde_json::json!({\n770\t            \"type\": \"object\",\n771\t            \"properties\": {\n772\t                \"bead_id\": {\n773\t                    \"type\": \"string\",\n774\t                    \"description\": \"The bead/task ID to assign\"\n775\t                },\n776\t                \"model\": {\n777\t                    \"type\": \"string\",\n778\t                    \"enum\": [\"sonnet\", \"opus\", \"haiku\", \"glm\"],\n779\t                    \"description\": \"Target model for the task\"\n780\t                }\n781\t            },\n782\t            \"required\": [\"bead_id\", \"model\"]\n783\t        })\n784\t    }\n785\t\n786\t    fn requires_confirmation(&self) -> bool {\n787\t        true\n788\t    }\n789\t\n790\t    async fn get_confirmation(\n791\t        &self,\n792\t        params: &serde_json::Value,\n793\t        context: &DashboardContext,\n794\t    ) -> Option<ActionConfirmation> {\n795\t        let bead_id = params.get(\"bead_id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str())?;\n796\t        let new_model = params.get(\"model\").and_then(|v| v.as_str())?;\n797\t\n798\t        // Find the task\n799\t        let task = context.tasks.iter().find(|t| t.id == bead_id)?;\n800\t\n801\t        // Estimate cost difference\n802\t        let cost_impact = match (task.assigned_model.as_deref(), new_model) {\n803\t            (Some(\"glm\"), \"opus\") => Some(22.50),\n804\t            (Some(\"sonnet\"), \"opus\") => Some(18.00),\n805\t            (Some(\"opus\"), \"sonnet\") => Some(-18.00),\n806\t            _ => None,\n807\t        };\n808\t\n809\t        Some(ActionConfirmation {\n810\t            title: format!(\"Reassign {} to {}?\", bead_id, new_model),\n811\t            description: format!(\n812\t                \"Task {} will be reassigned from {} to {}.\",\n813\t                bead_id,\n814\t                task.assigned_model.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"unassigned\"),\n815\t                new_model\n816\t            ),\n817\t            level: if task.in_progress {\n818\t                ConfirmationLevel::Danger\n819\t            } else {\n820\t                ConfirmationLevel::Info\n821\t            },\n822\t            cost_impact,\n823\t            affected_items: vec![bead_id.to_string()],\n824\t            reversible: true,\n825\t        })\n826\t    }\n827\t\n828\t    async fn execute(\n829\t        &self,\n830\t        params: serde_json::Value,\n831\t        _context: &DashboardContext,\n832\t    ) -> Result<ToolResult> {\n833\t        let bead_id = params\n834\t            .get(\"bead_id\")\n835\t            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n836\t            .ok_or_else(|| ChatError::ToolExecutionFailed(\"bead_id is required\".to_string()))?;\n837\t\n838\t        let model = Chat tools for read-only queries and actions.\n\nuse ::async_trait::async_trait;\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\n\nuse crate::context::DashboardContext;\nuse crate::error::{ChatError, Result};\n\n/// Trait for chat tools that can be invoked by the AI agent.\n#[async_trait]\npub trait ChatTool: Send + Sync {\n    /// Get the tool name.\n    fn name(&self) -> &str;\n\n    /// Get the tool description for the AI.\n    fn description(&self) -> &str;\n\n    /// Get the JSON schema for tool parameters.\n    fn parameters_schema(&self) -> serde_json::Value;\n\n    /// Whether this tool requires confirmation before execution.\n    fn requires_confirmation(&self) -> bool {\n        false\n    }\n\n    /// Execute the tool with the given parameters.\n    async fn execute(\n        &self,\n        params: serde_json::Value,\n        context: &DashboardContext,\n    ) -> Result<ToolResult>;\n\n    /// Get confirmation details for the action (if requires_confirmation is true).\n    async fn get_confirmation(\n        &self,\n        params: &serde_json::Value,\n        context: &DashboardContext,\n    ) -> Option<ActionConfirmation> {\n        let _ = (params, context);\n        None\n    }\n}\n\n/// Result from a tool execution.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ToolResult {\n    /// Whether the tool execution was successful.\n    pub success: bool,\n\n    /// The result data (JSON).\n    pub data: serde_json::Value,\n\n    /// Human-readable message.\n    pub message: String,\n\n    /// Any side effects that occurred.\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub side_effects: Vec<SideEffect>,\n}\n\nimpl ToolResult {\n    /// Create a successful result.\n    pub fn success(data: impl Serialize, message: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            success: true,\n            data: serde_json::to_value(data).unwrap_or(serde_json::Value::Null),\n            message: message.into(),\n            side_effects: vec![],\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a failed result.\n    pub fn error(message: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            success: false,\n            data: serde_json::Value::Null,\n            message: message.into(),\n            side_effects: vec![],\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Add a side effect.\n    pub fn with_side_effect(mut self, effect: SideEffect) -> Self {\n        self.side_effects.push(effect);\n        self\n    }\n}\n\n/// A side effect from a tool execution.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct SideEffect {\n    /// Type of side effect.\n    pub effect_type: String,\n\n    /// Description of what happened.\n    pub description: String,\n\n    /// Additional data.\n    pub data: Option<serde_json::Value>,\n}\n\n/// Confirmation details for an action.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ActionConfirmation {\n    /// Title of the confirmation dialog.\n    pub title: String,\n\n    /// Description of what will happen.\n    pub description: String,\n\n    /// Warning level (info, warning, danger).\n    pub level: ConfirmationLevel,\n\n    /// Estimated cost impact (if applicable).\n    pub cost_impact: Option<f64>,\n\n    /// Items that will be affected.\n    pub affected_items: Vec<String>,\n\n    /// Whether this action is reversible.\n    pub reversible: bool,\n}\n\n/// Confirmation level for actions.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\npub enum ConfirmationLevel {\n    /// Informational (no danger)\n    Info,\n    /// Warning (some risk)\n    Warning,\n    /// Danger (high risk, destructive)\n    Danger,\n}\n\n/// A tool call from the AI.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ToolCall {\n    /// Tool name.\n    pub name: String,\n\n    /// Tool parameters (JSON).\n    pub parameters: serde_json::Value,\n\n    /// Tool call ID (from the API).\n    pub id: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Registry of available tools.\npub struct ToolRegistry {\n    tools: HashMap<String, Arc<dyn ChatTool>>,\n}\n\nimpl ToolRegistry {\n    /// Create a new empty registry.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            tools: HashMap::new(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Register a tool.\n    pub fn register(&mut self, tool: impl ChatTool + 'static) {\n        let name = tool.name().to_string();\n        self.tools.insert(name, Arc::new(tool));\n    }\n\n    /// Get a tool by name.\n    pub fn get(&self, name: &str) -> Option<Arc<dyn ChatTool>> {\n        self.tools.get(name).cloned()\n    }\n\n    /// Get all tool definitions for the API.\n    pub fn tool_definitions(&self) -> Vec<ToolDefinition> {\n        self.tools\n            .values()\n            .map(|tool| ToolDefinition {\n                name: tool.name().to_string(),\n                description: tool.description().to_string(),\n                input_schema: tool.parameters_schema(),\n            })\n            .collect()\n    }\n\n    /// List all tool names.\n    pub fn tool_names(&self) -> Vec<&str> {\n        self.tools.keys().map(|s| s.as_str()).collect()\n    }\n\n    /// Execute a tool call.\n    pub async fn execute(&self, call: &ToolCall, context: &DashboardContext) -> Result<ToolResult> {\n        let tool = self\n            .get(&call.name)\n            .ok_or_else(|| ChatError::ToolNotFound(call.name.clone()))?;\n\n        // Check if confirmation is required\n        if tool.requires_confirmation()\n            && let Some(confirmation) = tool.get_confirmation(&call.parameters, context).await\n        {\n            return Err(ChatError::ConfirmationRequired(serde_json::to_string(\n                &confirmation,\n            )?));\n        }\n\n        tool.execute(call.parameters.clone(), context).await\n    }\n\n    /// Create a registry with all built-in tools.\n    pub fn with_builtin_tools() -> Self {\n        let mut registry = Self::new();\n\n        // Read-only tools\n        registry.register(GetWorkerStatusTool);\n        registry.register(GetTaskQueueTool);\n        registry.register(GetCostAnalyticsTool);\n        registry.register(GetSubscriptionUsageTool);\n        registry.register(GetActivityLogTool);\n\n        // Action tools\n        registry.register(SpawnWorkerTool);\n        registry.register(KillWorkerTool);\n        registry.register(AssignTaskTool);\n        registry.register(PauseWorkersTool);\n        registry.register(ResumeWorkersTool);\n\n        registry\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for ToolRegistry {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::with_builtin_tools()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Tool definition for the API.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ToolDefinition {\n    /// Tool name.\n    pub name: String,\n\n    /// Tool description.\n    pub description: String,\n\n    /// Input schema (JSON Schema).\n    pub input_schema: serde_json::Value,\n}\n\n// ============ Built-in Read-Only Tools ============\n\n/// Get worker status tool.\npub struct GetWorkerStatusTool;\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl ChatTool for GetWorkerStatusTool {\n    fn name(&self) -> &str {\n        \"get_worker_status\"\n    }\n\n    fn description(&self) -> &str {\n        \"Get the current status of all workers in the pool, including their health, activity, and assigned tasks.\"\n    }\n\n    fn parameters_schema(&self) -> serde_json::Value {\n        serde_json::json!({\n            \"type\": \"object\",\n            \"properties\": {\n                \"worker_id\": {\n                    \"type\": \"string\",\n                    \"description\": \"Optional: Filter by specific worker ID\"\n                },\n                \"status_filter\": {\n                    \"type\": \"string\",\n                    \"enum\": [\"all\", \"healthy\", \"idle\", \"unhealthy\"],\n                    \"description\": \"Filter workers by status\"\n                }\n            }\n        })\n    }\n\n    async fn execute(\n        &self,\n        params: serde_json::Value,\n        context: &DashboardContext,\n    ) -> Result<ToolResult> {\n        let worker_id = params.get(\"worker_id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str());\n        let status_filter = params\n            .get(\"status_filter\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n            .unwrap_or(\"all\");\n\n        let workers = if let Some(id) = worker_id {\n            context\n                .workers\n                .iter()\n                .filter(|w| w.session_name == id)\n                .cloned()\n                .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n        } else {\n            context.workers.clone()\n        };\n\n        let filtered: Vec<_> = workers\n            .into_iter()\n            .filter(|w| match status_filter {\n                \"healthy\" => w.is_healthy,\n                \"idle\" => w.is_idle,\n                \"unhealthy\" => !w.is_healthy,\n                _ => true,\n            })\n            .collect();\n\n        let total = context.workers.len();\n        let healthy = context.workers.iter().filter(|w| w.is_healthy).count();\n        let idle = context.workers.iter().filter(|w| w.is_idle).count();\n\n        Ok(ToolResult::success(\n            serde_json::json!({\n                \"total\": total,\n                \"healthy\": healthy,\n                \"idle\": idle,\n                \"unhealthy\": total - healthy,\n                \"workers\": filtered\n            }),\n            format!(\n                \"Found {} workers ({} healthy, {} idle)\",\n                total, healthy, idle\n            ),\n        ))\n    }\n}\n\n/// Get task queue tool.\npub struct GetTaskQueueTool;\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl ChatTool for GetTaskQueueTool {\n    fn name(&self) -> &str {\n        \"get_task_queue\"\n    }\n\n    fn description(&self) -> &str {\n        \"Get the current task queue with ready beads, including priority and assignment information.\"\n    }\n\n    fn parameters_schema(&self) -> serde_json::Value {\n        serde_json::json!({\n            \"type\": \"object\",\n            \"properties\": {\n                \"workspace\": {\n                    \"type\": \"string\",\n                    \"description\": \"Optional: Filter by workspace path\"\n                },\n                \"priority\": {\n                    \"type\": \"string\",\n                    \"enum\": [\"P0\", \"P1\", \"P2\", \"P3\", \"P4\"],\n                    \"description\": \"Optional: Filter by priority\"\n                },\n                \"limit\": {\n                    \"type\": \"integer\",\n                    \"description\": \"Maximum number of tasks to return (default: 20)\"\n                }\n            }\n        })\n    }\n\n    async fn execute(\n        &self,\n        params: serde_json::Value,\n        context: &DashboardContext,\n    ) -> Result<ToolResult> {\n        let workspace = params.get(\"workspace\").and_then(|v| v.as_str());\n        let priority = params.get(\"priority\").and_then(|v| v.as_str());\n        let limit = params.get(\"limit\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()).unwrap_or(20) as usize;\n\n        let mut tasks = context.tasks.clone();\n\n        if let Some(ws) = workspace {\n            tasks.retain(|t| t.workspace.contains(ws));\n        }\n\n        if let Some(p) = priority {\n            tasks.retain(|t| t.priority == p);\n        }\n\n        let total = tasks.len();\n        tasks.truncate(limit);\n\n        let in_progress = context.tasks.iter().filter(|t| t.in_progress).count();\n\n        Ok(ToolResult::success(\n            serde_json::json!({\n                \"total_ready\": total,\n                \"in_progress\": in_progress,\n                \"beads\": tasks\n            }),\n            format!(\"{} ready tasks, {} in progress\", total, in_progress),\n        ))\n    }\n}\n\n/// Get cost analytics tool.\npub struct GetCostAnalyticsTool;\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl ChatTool for GetCostAnalyticsTool {\n    fn name(&self) -> &str {\n        \"get_cost_analytics\"\n    }\n\n    fn description(&self) -> &str {\n        \"Get cost analytics including spending by model, time period, and projected costs.\"\n    }\n\n    fn parameters_schema(&self) -> serde_json::Value {\n        serde_json::json!({\n            \"type\": \"object\",\n            \"properties\": {\n                \"timeframe\": {\n                    \"type\": \"string\",\n                    \"enum\": [\"today\", \"yesterday\", \"week\", \"month\", \"projected\"],\n                    \"description\": \"Time period for cost analysis (default: today)\"\n                }\n            }\n        })\n    }\n\n    async fn execute(\n        &self,\n        params: serde_json::Value,\n        context: &DashboardContext,\n    ) -> Result<ToolResult> {\n        let timeframe = params\n            .get(\"timeframe\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n            .unwrap_or(\"today\");\n\n        // Return the cost analytics from context\n        let costs = match timeframe {\n            \"today\" => &context.costs_today,\n            \"projected\" => &context.costs_projected,\n            _ => &context.costs_today,\n        };\n\n        Ok(ToolResult::success(\n            costs,\n            format!(\"Cost analytics for {}\", timeframe),\n        ))\n    }\n}\n\n/// Get subscription usage tool.\npub struct GetSubscriptionUsageTool;\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl ChatTool for GetSubscriptionUsageTool {\n    fn name(&self) -> &str {\n        \"get_subscription_usage\"\n    }\n\n    fn description(&self) -> &str {\n        \"Get subscription usage and quota status for all configured subscriptions.\"\n    }\n\n    fn parameters_schema(&self) -> serde_json::Value {\n        serde_json::json!({\n            \"type\": \"object\",\n            \"properties\": {}\n        })\n    }\n\n    async fn execute(\n        &self,\n        _params: serde_json::Value,\n        context: &DashboardContext,\n    ) -> Result<ToolResult> {\n        Ok(ToolResult::success(\n            &context.subscriptions,\n            format!(\"{} subscriptions tracked\", context.subscriptions.len()),\n        ))\n    }\n}\n\n/// Get activity log tool.\npub struct GetActivityLogTool;\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl ChatTool for GetActivityLogTool {\n    fn name(&self) -> &str {\n        \"get_activity_log\"\n    }\n\n    fn description(&self) -> &str {\n        \"Get recent activity log entries including worker events, task completions, and errors.\"\n    }\n\n    fn parameters_schema(&self) -> serde_json::Value {\n        serde_json::json!({\n            \"type\": \"object\",\n            \"properties\": {\n                \"hours\": {\n                    \"type\": \"integer\",\n                    \"description\": \"Number of hours of history to retrieve (default: 1)\"\n                },\n                \"filter\": {\n                    \"type\": \"string\",\n                    \"enum\": [\"all\", \"spawns\", \"completions\", \"errors\"],\n                    \"description\": \"Filter by event type\"\n                }\n            }\n        })\n    }\n\n    async fn execute(\n        &self,\n        params: serde_json::Value,\n        context: &DashboardContext,\n    ) -> Result<ToolResult> {\n        let _hours = params.get(\"hours\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()).unwrap_or(1);\n        let filter = params\n            .get(\"filter\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n            .unwrap_or(\"all\");\n\n        let mut events = context.recent_events.clone();\n\n        if filter != \"all\" {\n            events.retain(|e| e.event_type == filter);\n        }\n\n        Ok(ToolResult::success(\n            serde_json::json!({\n                \"events\": events\n            }),\n            format!(\"{} events in activity log\", events.len()),\n        ))\n    }\n}\n\n// ============ Built-in Action Tools ============\n\n/// Spawn worker tool.\npub struct SpawnWorkerTool;\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl ChatTool for SpawnWorkerTool {\n    fn name(&self) -> &str {\n        \"spawn_worker\"\n    }\n\n    fn description(&self) -> &str {\n        \"Spawn one or more new workers with a specified model type.\"\n    }\n\n    fn parameters_schema(&self) -> serde_json::Value {\n        serde_json::json!({\n            \"type\": \"object\",\n            \"properties\": {\n                \"worker_type\": {\n                    \"type\": \"string\",\n                    \"enum\": [\"sonnet\", \"opus\", \"haiku\", \"glm\"],\n                    \"description\": \"Type of worker to spawn\"\n                },\n                \"count\": {\n                    \"type\": \"integer\",\n                    \"description\": \"Number of workers to spawn (default: 1)\"\n                },\n                \"workspace\": {\n                    \"type\": \"string\",\n                    \"description\": \"Optional: Workspace to assign workers to\"\n                }\n            },\n            \"required\": [\"worker_type\"]\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn requires_confirmation(&self) -> bool {\n        true\n    }\n\n    async fn get_confirmation(\n        &self,\n        params: &serde_json::Value,\n        _context: &DashboardContext,\n    ) -> Option<ActionConfirmation> {\n        let count = params.get(\"count\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()).unwrap_or(1);\n        let worker_type = params\n            .get(\"worker_type\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n            .unwrap_or(\"unknown\");\n\n        // Auto-confirm if spawning 2 or fewer workers\n        if count <= 2 {\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        Some(ActionConfirmation {\n            title: format!(\"Spawn {} {} workers?\", count, worker_type),\n            description: format!(\n                \"This will spawn {} new {} workers. This may increase costs.\",\n                count, worker_type\n            ),\n            level: ConfirmationLevel::Warning,\n            cost_impact: None,\n            affected_items: vec![format!(\"{} new workers\", count)],\n            reversible: true,\n        })\n    }\n\n    async fn execute(\n        &self,\n        params: serde_json::Value,\n        context: &DashboardContext,\n    ) -> Result<ToolResult> {\n        let worker_type = params\n            .get(\"worker_type\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n            .ok_or_else(|| ChatError::ToolExecutionFailed(\"worker_type is required\".to_string()))?;\n\n        let count = params.get(\"count\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()).unwrap_or(1) as usize;\n        let workspace = params.get(\"workspace\").and_then(|v| v.as_str());\n\n        // Check if worker spawner is available\n        let spawner = context.worker_spawner.as_ref()\n            .ok_or_else(|| ChatError::ToolExecutionFailed(\n                \"Worker spawning not available. Please use the FORGE TUI to spawn workers.\".to_string()\n            ))?;\n\n        // Spawn workers using the real spawner\n        let workspace_path = workspace.map(std::path::PathBuf::from);\n        match spawner.spawn_workers(worker_type, count, workspace_path.as_ref()).await {\n            Ok(spawned) => {\n                let mut result = ToolResult::success(\n                    serde_json::json!({\n                        \"spawned\": spawned,\n                        \"count\": spawned.len(),\n                        \"worker_type\": worker_type,\n                        \"workspace\": workspace\n                    }),\n                    format!(\"Spawned {} {} worker(s)\", spawned.len(), worker_type),\n                );\n\n                for s in &spawned {\n                    result = result.with_side_effect(SideEffect {\n                        effect_type: \"spawn\".to_string(),\n                        description: format!(\"Spawned worker {} (PID: {})\", s.session_name, s.pid),\n                        data: Some(serde_json::json!({\n                            \"session\": s.session_name,\n                            \"worker_id\": s.worker_id,\n                            \"pid\": s.pid\n                        })),\n                    });\n                }\n\n                Ok(result)\n            }\n            Err(e) => Ok(ToolResult::error(format!(\"Failed to spawn workers: {}\", e)))\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Kill worker tool.\npub struct KillWorkerTool;\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl ChatTool for KillWorkerTool {\n    fn name(&self) -> &str {\n        \"kill_worker\"\n    }\n\n    fn description(&self) -> &str {\n        \"Kill/terminate a specific worker by session name.\"\n    }\n\n    fn parameters_schema(&self) -> serde_json::Value {\n        serde_json::json!({\n            \"type\": \"object\",\n            \"properties\": {\n                \"session_name\": {\n                    \"type\": \"string\",\n                    \"description\": \"The session name of the worker to kill\"\n                }\n            },\n            \"required\": [\"session_name\"]\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn requires_confirmation(&self) -> bool {\n        true\n    }\n\n    async fn get_confirmation(\n        &self,\n        params: &serde_json::Value,\n        context: &DashboardContext,\n    ) -> Option<ActionConfirmation> {\n        let session_name = params.get(\"session_name\").and_then(|v| v.as_str())?;\n\n        // Find the worker\n        let worker = context\n            .workers\n            .iter()\n            .find(|w| w.session_name == session_name)?;\n\n        Some(ActionConfirmation {\n            title: format!(\"Kill worker {}?\", session_name),\n            description: if worker.current_task.is_some() {\n                format!(\n                    \"Worker {} is currently working on a task. Killing it will interrupt the task.\",\n                    session_name\n                )\n            } else {\n                format!(\"Worker {} will be terminated.\", session_name)\n            },\n            level: if worker.current_task.is_some() {\n                ConfirmationLevel::Danger\n            } else {\n                ConfirmationLevel::Warning\n            },\n            cost_impact: None,\n            affected_items: vec![session_name.to_string()],\n            reversible: true,\n        })\n    }\n\n    async fn execute(\n        &self,\n        params: serde_json::Value,\n        _context: &DashboardContext,\n    ) -> Result<ToolResult> {\n        let session_name = params\n            .get(\"session_name\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n            .ok_or_else(|| {\n                ChatError::ToolExecutionFailed(\"session_name is required\".to_string())\n            })?;\n\n        // In a real implementation, this would call tmux to kill the session\n        Ok(ToolResult::success(\n            serde_json::json!({\n                \"killed\": session_name\n            }),\n            format!(\"Killed worker {}\", session_name),\n        )\n        .with_side_effect(SideEffect {\n            effect_type: \"kill\".to_string(),\n            description: format!(\"Killed worker {}\", session_name),\n            data: Some(serde_json::json!({\"session\": session_name})),\n        }))\n    }\n}\n\n/// Assign task tool.\npub struct AssignTaskTool;\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl ChatTool for AssignTaskTool {\n    fn name(&self) -> &str {\n        \"assign_task\"\n    }\n\n    fn description(&self) -> &str {\n        \"Assign or reassign a task/bead to a specific model tier.\"\n    }\n\n    fn parameters_schema(&self) -> serde_json::Value {\n        serde_json::json!({\n            \"type\": \"object\",\n            \"properties\": {\n                \"bead_id\": {\n                    \"type\": \"string\",\n                    \"description\": \"The bead/task ID to assign\"\n                },\n                \"model\": {\n                    \"type\": \"string\",\n                    \"enum\": [\"sonnet\", \"opus\", \"haiku\", \"glm\"],\n                    \"description\": \"Target model for the task\"\n                }\n            },\n            \"required\": [\"bead_id\", \"model\"]\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn requires_confirmation(&self) -> bool {\n        true\n    }\n\n    async fn get_confirmation(\n        &self,\n        params: &serde_json::Value,\n        context: &DashboardContext,\n    ) -> Option<ActionConfirmation> {\n        let bead_id = params.get(\"bead_id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str())?;\n        let new_model = params.get(\"model\").and_then(|v| v.as_str())?;\n\n        // Find the task\n        let task = context.tasks.iter().find(|t| t.id == bead_id)?;\n\n        // Estimate cost difference\n        let cost_impact = match (task.assigned_model.as_deref(), new_model) {\n            (Some(\"glm\"), \"opus\") => Some(22.50),\n            (Some(\"sonnet\"), \"opus\") => Some(18.00),\n            (Some(\"opus\"), \"sonnet\") => Some(-18.00),\n            _ => None,\n        };\n\n        Some(ActionConfirmation {\n            title: format!(\"Reassign {} to {}?\", bead_id, new_model),\n            description: format!(\n                \"Task {} will be reassigned from {} to {}.\",\n                bead_id,\n                task.assigned_model.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"unassigned\"),\n                new_model\n            ),\n            level: if task.in_progress {\n                ConfirmationLevel::Danger\n            } else {\n                ConfirmationLevel::Info\n            },\n            cost_impact,\n            affected_items: vec![bead_id.to_string()],\n            reversible: true,\n        })\n    }\n\n    async fn execute(\n        &self,\n        params: serde_json::Value,\n        _context: &DashboardContext,\n    ) -> Result<ToolResult> {\n        let bead_id = params\n            .get(\"bead_id\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n            .ok_or_else(|| ChatError::ToolExecutionFailed(\"bead_id is required\".to_string()))?;\n\n        let model = params\n            .get(\"model\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n            .ok_or_else(|| ChatError::ToolExecutionFailed(\"model is required\".to_string()))?;\n\n        Ok(ToolResult::success(\n            serde_json::json!({\n                \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n                \"new_model\": model,\n                \"status\": \"reassigned\"\n            }),\n            format!(\"Reassigned {} to {}\", bead_id, model),\n        )\n        .with_side_effect(SideEffect {\n            effect_type: \"assign\".to_string(),\n            description: format!(\"Assigned {} to {}\", bead_id, model),\n            data: Some(serde_json::json!({\"bead_id\": bead_id, \"model\": model})),\n        }))\n    }\n}\n\n/// Pause workers tool.\npub struct PauseWorkersTool;\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl ChatTool for PauseWorkersTool {\n    fn name(&self) -> &str {\n        \"pause_workers\"\n    }\n\n    fn description(&self) -> &str {\n        \"Pause all workers for a specified duration.\"\n    }\n\n    fn parameters_schema(&self) -> serde_json::Value {\n        serde_json::json!({\n            \"type\": \"object\",\n            \"properties\": {\n                \"duration_minutes\": {\n                    \"type\": \"integer\",\n                    \"description\": \"Duration to pause in minutes (default: 5)\"\n                }\n            }\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn requires_confirmation(&self) -> bool {\n        true\n    }\n\n    async fn get_confirmation(\n        &self,\n        params: &serde_json::Value,\n        context: &DashboardContext,\n    ) -> Option<ActionConfirmation> {\n        let duration = params\n            .get(\"duration_minutes\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n            .unwrap_or(5);\n\n        // Only require confirmation for long pauses\n        if duration <= 10 {\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        let active_count = context.workers.iter().filter(|w| !w.is_idle).count();\n\n        Some(ActionConfirmation {\n            title: format!(\"Pause all workers for {} minutes?\", duration),\n            description: format!(\n                \"This will pause {} workers. {} are currently active and will be interrupted.\",\n                context.workers.len(),\n                active_count\n            ),\n            level: ConfirmationLevel::Warning,\n            cost_impact: None,\n            affected_items: context\n                .workers\n                .iter()\n                .map(|w| w.session_name.clone())\n                .collect(),\n            reversible: true,\n        })\n    }\n\n    async fn execute(\n        &self,\n        params: serde_json::Value,\n        context: &DashboardContext,\n    ) -> Result<ToolResult> {\n        let duration = params\n            .get(\"duration_minutes\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n            .unwrap_or(5);\n\n        Ok(ToolResult::success(\n            serde_json::json!({\n                \"paused\": context.workers.len(),\n                \"duration_minutes\": duration,\n                \"resume_at\": chrono::Utc::now() + chrono::Duration::minutes(duration as i64)\n            }),\n            format!(\n                \"Paused {} workers for {} minutes\",\n                context.workers.len(),\n                duration\n            ),\n        ))\n    }\n}\n\n/// Resume {\n265\t    /// Gather the current dashboard context.\n266\t    async fn gather(&self) -> Result<DashboardContext>;\n267\t}\n268\t\n269\t/// Context provider that caches context for a short duration.\n270\tpub struct ContextProvider {\n271\t    source: Arc<dyn ContextSource>,\n272\t    cache: RwLock<Option<CachedContext>>,\n273\t    cache_duration_secs: u64,\n274\t}\n275\t\n276\tstruct 5, // 5 second cache by default\n288\t        }\n289\t    }\n290\t\n291\t    /// Create with custom cache duration.\n292\t    pub fn with_cache_duration(mut self, secs: u64) -> Self {\n293\t        self.cache_duration_secs = secs;\n294\t        self\n295\t    }\n296\t\n297\t    /// Get the current context (from cache if fresh).\n298\t    pub async fn get_context(&self) -> Result<DashboardContext> {\n299\t        // Check cache\n300\t        {\n301\t            let cache = self.cache.read().await;\n302\t            if let Some(cached) = cache.as_ref()\n303\t                && cached.cached_at.elapsed().as_secs() < self.cache_duration_secs\n304\t            {\n305\t                return Ok(cached.context.clone());\n306\t            }\n307\t        }\n308\t\n309\t        // Gather fresh context\n310\t        let context = self.source.gather().await?;\n311\t\n312\t        // Update cache\n313\t        {\n314\t            let mut cache = self.cache.write().await;\n315\t            *cache = Some(CachedContext {\n316\t                context: context.clone(),\n317\t                cached_at: std::time::Instant::now(),\n318\t            });\n319\t        }\n320\t\n321\t        Ok(context)\n322\t    }\n323\t\n324\t    /// Force refresh the context.\n325\t    pub async fn refresh(&self) -> Result<DashboardContext> {\n326\t        let context = self.source.gather().await?;\n327\t\n328\t        {\n329\t            let mut cache = self.cache.write().await;\n330\t            *cache = Some(CachedContext {\n331\t                context: context.clone(),\n332\t                cached_at: std::time::Instant::now(),\n333\t            });\n334\t        }\n335\t\n336\t        Ok(context)\n337\t    }\n338\t\n339\t    /// Invalidate the cache.\n340\t    pub async fn invalidate(&self) {\n341\t        let mut cache = self.cache.write().await;\n342\t        *cache = None;\n343\t    }\n344\t}\n345\t\n346\t/// Mock context source for log\n461\tpub struct RealContextSource {\n462\t    /// Path to the forge status directory (~/.forge/status/)\n463\t    status_dir: PathBuf,\n464\t    /// Path to the cost database (~/.forge/costs.db)\n465\t    cost_db_path: PathBuf,\n466\t    /// Path to the subscriptions config (~/.forge/subscriptions.yaml)\n467\t    subscriptions_path: PathBuf,\n468\t    /// Path to the chat PathBuf,\n472\t}\n473\t\n474\timpl RealContextSource {\n475\t    /// Create a new real context source with default paths.\n476\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n477\t        let home = std::env::var(\"HOME\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"/home/coder\".to_string());\n478\t        let forge_dir = PathBuf::from(&home).join(\".forge\");\n479\t\n480\t        // Get workspace from environment or current directory\n481\t        let workspace = std::env::var(\"FORGE_WORKSPACE\")\n482\t            .map(PathBuf::from)\n483\t            .unwrap_or_else(|_| {\n484\t                std::env::current_dir().unwrap_or_else(|_| PathBuf::from(&home))\n485\t            });\n486\t\n487\t        Self {\n488\t            status_dir: forge_dir.join(\"status\"),\n489\t            cost_db_path: forge_dir.join(\"costs.db\"),\n490\t            subscriptions_path: forge_dir.join(\"subscriptions.yaml\"),\n491\t            audit_log_path: forge_dir.join(\"chat-audit.jsonl\"),\n492\t            workspace,\n493\t        }\n494\t    }\n495\t\n496\t    /// Create with custom paths (for testing).\n497\t    pub fn with_paths(\n498\t        status_dir: PathBuf,\n499\t        cost_db_path: PathBuf,\n500\t        subscriptions_path: PathBuf,\n501\t        audit_log_path: PathBuf,\n502\t        workspace: PathBuf,\n503\t    ) -> Self {\n504\t        Self {\n505\t            status_dir,\n506\t            cost_db_path,\n507\t            subscriptions_path,\n508\t            audit_log_path,\n509\t            workspace,\n510\t        }\n511\t    }\n512\t\n513\t    /// Read worker status files from the status directory.\n514\t    async fn read_workers(&self) -> Vec<WorkerInfo> {\n515\t        let mut workers = Vec::new();\n516\t\n517\t        // Read status directory\n518\t        let status_dir = self.status_dir.clone();\n519\t        let entries = match tokio::fs::read_dir(&status_dir).await {\n520\t            Ok(entries) => entries,\n521\t            Err(e) => {\n522\t                debug!(\"Failed to read status directory {:?}: {}\", status_dir, e);\n523\t                return workers;\n524\t            }\n525\t        };\n526\t\n527\t        let mut entries = entries;\n528\t        while let Ok(Some(entry)) = entries.next_entry().await {\n529\t            let path = entry.path();\n530\t            if path.extension().is_some_and(|ext| ext == \"json\") {\n531\t                match self.read_worker_status(&path).await {\n532\t                    Ok(worker) => workers.push(worker),\n533\t                    Err(e) => {\n534\t                        debug!(\"Failed to read worker status from {:?}: {}\", path, e);\n535\t                    }\n536\t                }\n537\t            }\n538\t        }\n539\t\n540\t        workers\n541\t    }\n542\t\n543\t    /// Read a single worker status file.\n544\t    async fn read_worker_status(&self, path: &PathBuf) -> std::io::Result<WorkerInfo> {\n545\t        let content = tokio::fs::read_to_string(path).await?;\n546\t        let status: WorkerStatusFile = serde_json::from_str(&content)?;\n547\t\n548\t        // Parse timestamps\n549\t        let started_at = status.started_at.as_ref().and_then(|s| {\n550\t            DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339(s)\n551\t                .map(|dt| dt.with_timezone(&Utc))\n552\t                .ok()\n553\t        });\n554\t\n555\t        let last_activity = status.last_activity.as_ref().and_then(|s| {\n556\t            DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339(s)\n557\t                .map(|dt| dt.with_timezone(&Utc))\n558\t                .ok()\n559\t        });\n560\t\n561\t        // Calculate uptime\n562\t        let uptime_secs = if let Some(started) = started_at {\n563\t            (Utc::now() - started).num_seconds().max(0) as u64\n564\t        } else {\n565\t            0\n566\t        };\n567\t\n568\t        // Determine worker type from model name\n569\t        let worker_type = if status.model.contains(\"sonnet\") {\n570\t            \"sonnet\"\n571\t        } else if status.model.contains(\"opus\") {\n572\t            \"opus\"\n573\t        } else if status.model.contains(\"haiku\") {\n574\t            \"haiku\"\n575\t        } else if status.model.contains(\"glm\") {\n576\t            \"glm\"\n577\t        } else {\n578\t            \"unknown\"\n579\t        }\n580\t        .to_string();\n581\t\n582\t        // Check if healthy (active or idle status)\n583\t        let is_healthy = matches!(\n584\t            status.status.as_str(),\n585\t            \"active\" | \"idle\" | \"starting\"\n586\t        );\n587\t\n588\t        Ok(WorkerInfo {\n589\t            session_name: status.worker_id,\n590\t            worker_type,\n591\t            workspace: status.workspace.unwrap_or_default(),\n592\t            is_healthy,\n593\t            is_idle: status.status == \"idle\",\n594\t            current_task: status.current_task,\n595\t            uptime_secs,\n596\t            last_activity,\n597\t            beads_completed: status.tasks_completed.unwrap_or(0) as u32,\n598\t        })\n599\t    }\n600\t\n601\t    /// Read task queue from beads via br CLI.\n602\t    async fn read_tasks(&self) -> Vec<TaskInfo> {\n603\t        let mut tasks = Vec::new();\n604\t\n605\t        // Use br CLI to get ready tasks\n606\t        let output = tokio::process::Command::new(\"br\")\n607\t            .args([\"ready\", \"--format\", \"json\"])\n608\t            .current_dir(&self.workspace)\n609\t            .output()\n610\t            .await;\n611\t\n612\t        if let Ok(output) = output\n613\t            && output.status.success()\n614\t        {\n615\t            let stdout = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout);\n616\t            if !stdout.trim().is_empty()\n617\t                && stdout.trim() != \"[]\"\n618\t                && let Ok(beads) = serde_json::from_str::<Vec<BeadJson>>(&stdout)\n619\t            {\n620\t                for bead in beads {\n621\t                    tasks.push(TaskInfo {\n622\t                        id: bead.id,\n623\t                        title: bead.title,\n624\t                        priority: format!(\"P{}\", bead.priority),\n625\t                        workspace: self.workspace.to_string_lossy().to_string(),\n626\t                        in_progress: false,\n627\t                        assigned_model: bead.assignee,\n628\t                        estimated_tokens: None,\n629\t                    });\n630\t                }\n631\t            }\n632\t        }\n633\t\n634\t        // Also get in-progress tasks\n635\t        let output = tokio::process::Command::new(\"br\")\n636\t            .args([\"list\", \"--status\", \"in_progress\", \"--format\", \"json\"])\n637\t            .current_dir(&self.workspace)\n638\t 0.0\n702\t                    },\n703\t                })\n704\t                .collect(),\n705\t            by_priority: vec![], // Not tracked in forge-cost\n706\t        };\n707\t\n708\t        // Context injection for dashboard state.\n\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::fmt;\nuse std::path::PathBuf;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\nuse tracing::{debug, warn};\n\nuse crate::error::Result;\nuse crate::spawner::WorkerSpawner;\n\n/// Dashboard context for tool execution.\n///\n/// This structure provides the current state of the dashboard to tools,\n/// including worker status, task queue, costs, and subscriptions.\n#[derive(Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct DashboardContext {\n    /// Current workers in the pool.\n    pub workers: Vec<WorkerInfo>,\n\n    /// Current task queue.\n    pub tasks: Vec<TaskInfo>,\n\n    /// Today's cost analytics.\n    pub costs_today: CostAnalytics,\n\n    /// Projected costs for the month.\n    pub costs_projected: CostAnalytics,\n\n    /// Subscription usage.\n    pub subscriptions: Vec<SubscriptionInfo>,\n\n    /// Recent events.\n    pub recent_events: Vec<EventInfo>,\n\n    /// Context timestamp.\n    pub timestamp: DateTime<Utc>,\n\n    /// Optional worker spawner for action tools.\n    ///\n    /// This is excluded from serialization (serde skip) because it's a runtime\n    /// reference that cannot be serialized. When the context is serialized\n    /// (e.g., for audit logging), this field is skipped.\n    #[serde(skip)]\n    pub worker_spawner: Option<Arc<dyn WorkerSpawner>>,\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Debug for DashboardContext {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        f.debug_struct(\"DashboardContext\")\n            .field(\"workers\", &self.workers)\n            .field(\"tasks\", &self.tasks)\n            .field(\"costs_today\", &self.costs_today)\n            .field(\"costs_projected\", &self.costs_projected)\n            .field(\"subscriptions\", &self.subscriptions)\n            .field(\"recent_events\", &self.recent_events)\n            .field(\"timestamp\", &self.timestamp)\n            .field(\"worker_spawner\", &self.worker_spawner.as_ref().map(|_| \"<WorkerSpawner>\"))\n            .finish()\n    }\n}\n\nimpl DashboardContext {\n    /// Create a new empty context.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            timestamp: Utc::now(),\n            worker_spawner: None,\n            ..Default::default()\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Set the worker spawner.\n    pub fn with_worker_spawner(mut self, spawner: Arc<dyn WorkerSpawner>) -> Self {\n        self.worker_spawner = Some(spawner);\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Format context as a summary for the AI prompt.\n    pub fn to_summary(&self) -> String {\n        let mut summary = String::new();\n\n        // Workers summary\n        let healthy = self.workers.iter().filter(|w| w.is_healthy).count();\n        let idle = self.workers.iter().filter(|w| w.is_idle).count();\n        summary.push_str(&format!(\n            \"Workers: {} total ({} healthy, {} idle)\\n\",\n            self.workers.len(),\n            healthy,\n            idle\n        ));\n\n        // Tasks summary\n        let in_progress = self.tasks.iter().filter(|t| t.in_progress).count();\n        summary.push_str(&format!(\n            \"Tasks: {} ready, {} in progress\\n\",\n            self.tasks.len(),\n            in_progress\n        ));\n\n        // Costs summary\n        summary.push_str(&format!(\n            \"Costs today: ${:.2}\\n\",\n            self.costs_today.total_cost_usd\n        ));\n\n        // Subscriptions summary\n        for sub in &self.subscriptions {\n            if let Some(limit) = sub.quota_limit {\n                summary.push_str(&format!(\n                    \"{}: {}/{} ({:.0}%)\\n\",\n                    sub.name,\n                    sub.quota_used,\n                    limit,\n                    (sub.quota_used as f64 / limit as f64) * 100.0\n                ));\n            }\n        }\n\n        summary\n    }\n}\n\n/// Worker information for context.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct WorkerInfo {\n    /// Session name.\n    pub session_name: String,\n\n    /// Worker type (e.g., \"sonnet\", \"opus\", \"glm\").\n    pub worker_type: String,\n\n    /// Workspace path.\n    pub workspace: String,\n\n    /// Whether the worker is healthy.\n    pub is_healthy: bool,\n\n    /// Whether the worker is idle.\n    pub is_idle: bool,\n\n    /// Current task (if any).\n    pub current_task: Option<String>,\n\n    /// Uptime in seconds.\n    pub uptime_secs: u64,\n\n    /// Last activity timestamp.\n    pub last_activity: {\n    /// Task/bead ID.\n    pub id: String,\n\n    /// Task title.\n    pub title: String,\n\n    /// Priority (P0-P4).\n    pub priority: String,\n\n    /// Workspace path.\n    pub workspace: Vec<PriorityCost>,\n}\n\n/// Cost breakdown by model.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ModelCost {\n    /// Model name.\n    pub model: String,\n\n    /// Cost in USD.\n    pub cost_usd: f64,\n\n    /// Percentage of total.\n    pub percentage: f64,\n}\n\n/// Cost breakdown by priority.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Provider for dashboard context.\n///\n/// This trait allows different implementations for gathering context,\n/// such as live data from the system or mock data for testing.\n#[async_trait::async_trait]\npub trait ContextSource: Send + Sync {\n    /// Gather the current dashboard context.\n    async fn gather(&self) -> Result<DashboardContext>;\n}\n\n/// Context provider that caches context for a short duration.\npub struct ContextProvider {\n    source: Arc<dyn ContextSource>,\n    cache: RwLock<Option<CachedContext>>,\n    cache_duration_secs: u64,\n}\n\nstruct CachedContext {\n    context: DashboardContext,\n    cached_at: std::time::Instant,\n}\n\nimpl ContextProvider {\n    /// Create a new context provider.\n    pub fn new(source: impl ContextSource + 'static) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            source: Arc::new(source),\n            cache: RwLock::new(None),\n            cache_duration_secs: 5, // 5 second cache by default\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create with custom cache duration.\n    pub fn with_cache_duration(mut self, secs: u64) -> Self {\n        self.cache_duration_secs = secs;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Get the current context (from cache if fresh).\n    pub async fn get_context(&self) -> Result<DashboardContext> {\n        // Check cache\n        {\n            let cache = self.cache.read().await;\n            if let Some(cached) = cache.as_ref()\n                && cached.cached_at.elapsed().as_secs() < self.cache_duration_secs\n            {\n                return Ok(cached.context.clone());\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Gather fresh context\n        let context = self.source.gather().await?;\n\n        // Update cache\n        {\n            let mut cache = self.cache.write().await;\n            *cache = Some(CachedContext {\n                context: context.clone(),\n                cached_at: std::time::Instant::now(),\n            });\n        }\n\n        Ok(context)\n    }\n\n    /// Force refresh the context.\n    pub async fn refresh(&self) -> Result<DashboardContext> {\n        let context = self.source.gather().await?;\n\n        {\n            let mut cache = self.cache.write().await;\n            *cache = Some(CachedContext {\n                context: context.clone(),\n                cached_at: std::time::Instant::now(),\n            });\n        }\n\n        Ok(context)\n    }\n\n    /// Invalidate the cache.\n    pub async fn invalidate(&self) {\n        let mut cache = self.cache.write().await;\n        *cache = None;\n    }\n}\n\n/// Mock context source for testing.\npub struct MockContextSource {\n    context: DashboardContext,\n}\n\nimpl MockContextSource {\n    /// Create a new mock context source.\n    pub fn Ok(self.context.clone())\n    }\n}\n\n/// Real context source that reads live data from the dashboard.\n///\n/// This implementation reads:\n/// - Worker status from ~/.forge/status/*.json\n/// - Task queue from the workspace's .beads/ directory via `br` CLI\n/// - Cost data from ~/.forge/costs.db\n/// - Recent events from the chat audit log\npub struct RealContextSource {\n    /// Path to the forge status directory (~/.forge/status/)\n    status_dir: PathBuf,\n    /// Path to the cost database (~/.forge/costs.db)\n    cost_db_path: PathBuf,\n    /// Path to the subscriptions config (~/.forge/subscriptions.yaml)\n    subscriptions_path: PathBuf,\n    /// Path to the chat audit log (~/.forge/chat-audit.jsonl)\n    audit_log_path: PathBuf,\n    /// Workspace path for beads (current working directory or FORGE_WORKSPACE)\n    workspace: PathBuf,\n}\n\nimpl RealContextSource {\n    /// Create a new real context source with default paths.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        let home = std::env::var(\"HOME\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"/home/coder\".to_string());\n        let forge_dir = PathBuf::from(&home).join(\".forge\");\n\n        // Get workspace from environment or current directory\n        let workspace = std::env::var(\"FORGE_WORKSPACE\")\n            .map(PathBuf::from)\n            .unwrap_or_else(|_| {\n                std::env::current_dir().unwrap_or_else(|_| PathBuf::from(&home))\n            });\n\n        Self {\n            status_dir: forge_dir.join(\"status\"),\n            cost_db_path: forge_dir.join(\"costs.db\"),\n            subscriptions_path: forge_dir.join(\"subscriptions.yaml\"),\n            audit_log_path: forge_dir.join(\"chat-audit.jsonl\"),\n            workspace,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create with custom paths (for testing).\n    pub fn with_paths(\n        status_dir: PathBuf,\n        cost_db_path: PathBuf,\n        subscriptions_path: PathBuf,\n        audit_log_path: PathBuf,\n        workspace: PathBuf,\n    ) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            status_dir,\n            cost_db_path,\n            subscriptions_path,\n            audit_log_path,\n            workspace,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Read worker status files from the status directory.\n    async fn read_workers(&self) -> Vec<WorkerInfo> {\n        let mut workers = Vec::new();\n\n        // Read status directory\n        let status_dir = self.status_dir.clone();\n        let entries = match tokio::fs::read_dir(&status_dir).await {\n            Ok(entries) => entries,\n            Err(e) => {\n                debug!(\"Failed to read status directory {:?}: {}\", status_dir, e);\n                return workers;\n            }\n        };\n\n        let mut entries = entries;\n        while let Ok(Some(entry)) = entries.next_entry().await {\n            let path = entry.path();\n            if path.extension().is_some_and(|ext| ext == \"json\") {\n                match self.read_worker_status(&path).await {\n                    Ok(worker) => workers.push(worker),\n                    Err(e) => {\n                        debug!(\"Failed to read worker status from {:?}: {}\", path, e);\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        workers\n    }\n\n    /// Read a single worker status file.\n    async fn read_worker_status(&self, path: &PathBuf) -> std::io::Result<WorkerInfo> {\n        let content = tokio::fs::read_to_string(path).await?;\n        let status: WorkerStatusFile = serde_json::from_str(&content)?;\n\n        // Parse timestamps\n        let started_at = status.started_at.as_ref().and_then(|s| {\n            DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339(s)\n                .map(|dt| dt.with_timezone(&Utc))\n                .ok()\n        });\n\n        let last_activity = status.last_activity.as_ref().and_then(|s| {\n            DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339(s)\n                .map(|dt| dt.with_timezone(&Utc))\n                .ok()\n        });\n\n        // Calculate uptime\n        let uptime_secs = if let Some(started) = started_at {\n            (Utc::now() - started).num_seconds().max(0) as u64\n        } else {\n            0\n        };\n\n        // Determine worker type from model name\n        let worker_type = if status.model.contains(\"sonnet\") {\n            \"sonnet\"\n        } else if status.model.contains(\"opus\") {\n            \"opus\"\n        } else if status.model.contains(\"haiku\") {\n            \"haiku\"\n        } else if status.model.contains(\"glm\") {\n            \"glm\"\n        } else {\n            \"unknown\"\n        }\n        .to_string();\n\n        // Check if healthy (active or idle status)\n        let is_healthy = matches!(\n            status.status.as_str(),\n            \"active\" | \"idle\" | \"starting\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(WorkerInfo {\n            session_name: status.worker_id,\n            worker_type,\n            workspace: status.workspace.unwrap_or_default(),\n            is_healthy,\n            is_idle: status.status == \"idle\",\n            current_task: status.current_task,\n            uptime_secs,\n            last_activity,\n            beads_completed: status.tasks_completed.unwrap_or(0) as u32,\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Read task queue from beads via br CLI.\n    async fn read_tasks(&self) -> Vec<TaskInfo> {\n        let mut tasks = Vec::new();\n\n        // Use br CLI to get ready tasks\n        let output = tokio::process::Command::new(\"br\")\n            .args([\"ready\", \"--format\", \"json\"])\n            .current_dir(&self.workspace)\n            .output()\n            .await;\n\n        if let Ok(output) = output\n            && output.status.success()\n        {\n            let stdout = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout);\n            if !stdout.trim().is_empty()\n                && stdout.trim() != \"[]\"\n                && let Ok(beads) = serde_json::from_str::<Vec<BeadJson>>(&stdout)\n            {\n                for bead in beads {\n                    tasks.push(TaskInfo {\n                        id: bead.id,\n                        title: bead.title,\n                        priority: format!(\"P{}\", bead.priority),\n                        workspace: self.workspace.to_string_lossy().to_string(),\n                        in_progress: false,\n                        assigned_model: bead.assignee,\n                        estimated_tokens: None,\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Also get in-progress tasks\n        let output = tokio::process::Command::new(\"br\")\n            .args([\"list\", \"--status\", \"in_progress\", \"--format\", \"json\"])\n            .current_dir(&self.workspace)\n            .output()\n            .await;\n\n        if let Ok(output) = output\n            && output.status.success()\n        {\n            let stdout = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout);\n            if !stdout.trim().is_empty()\n                && stdout.trim() != \"[]\"\n                && let Ok(beads) = serde_json::from_str::<Vec<BeadJson>>(&stdout)\n            {\n                for bead in beads {\n                    tasks.push(TaskInfo {\n                        id: bead.id,\n                        title: bead.title,\n                        priority: format!(\"P{}\", bead.priority),\n                        workspace: self.workspace.to_string_lossy().to_string(),\n                        in_progress: true,\n                        assigned_model: bead.assignee,\n                        estimated_tokens: None,\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        tasks\n    }\n\n    /// Read cost data from the database.\n    async fn read_costs(&self) -> (CostAnalytics, CostAnalytics) {\n        // Use forge-cost to query costs\n        let db = match forge_cost::CostDatabase::open(&self.cost_db_path) {\n            Ok(db) => db,\n            Err(e) => {\n                debug!(\"Failed to open cost database: {}\", e);\n                return (CostAnalytics::default(), CostAnalytics::default());\n            }\n        };\n\n        let query = forge_cost::CostQuery::new(&db);\n\n        // Get today's costs\n        let today = query.get_today_costs().unwrap_or_else(|_| {\n            forge_cost::DailyCost {\n                date: chrono::Utc::now().date_naive(),\n                total_cost_usd: 0.0,\n                call_count: 0,\n                total_tokens: 0,\n                by_model: vec![],\n            }\n        });\n        let costs_today = CostAnalytics {\n            timeframe: \"today\".to_string(),\n            total_cost_usd: today.total_cost_usd,\n            by_model: today\n                .by_model\n                .iter()\n                .map(|b| ModelCost {\n                    model: b.model.clone(),\n                    cost_usd: b.total_cost_usd,\n                    percentage: if today.total_cost_usd > 0.0 {\n                        (b.total_cost_usd / today.total_cost_usd) * 100.0\n                    } else {\n                        0.0\n                    },\n                })\n                .collect(),\n            by_priority: vec![], // Not tracked in forge-cost\n        };\n\n        // Get projected costs\n        let projected = query.get_projected_costs(None).unwrap_or(forge_cost::ProjectedCost {\n            current_total: 0.0,\n            daily_rate: 0.0,\n            days_remaining: 0,\n            projected_total: 0.0,\n            confidence: 0.0,\n        });\n        let costs_projected = CostAnalytics {\n            timeframe: \"month\".to_string(),\n            total_cost_usd: projected.projected_total,\n            by_model: vec![],\n            by_priority: vec![],\n        };\n\n        (costs_today, costs_projected)\n    }\n\n    /// Read subscription data from config.\n    async fn read_subscriptions(&self) -> Vec<SubscriptionInfo> {\n        let content = match tokio::fs::read_to_string(&self.subscriptions_path).await {\n            Ok(c) => c,\n            Err(e) => {\n                debug!(\"Failed to read subscriptions config: {}\", e);\n                return vec![];\n            }\n        };\n\n        let config: SubscriptionsConfig = match serde_yaml::from_str(&content) {\n            Ok(c) => c,\n            Err(e) => {\n                warn!(\"Failed to parse subscriptions config: {}\", e);\n                return vec![];\n            }\n        };\n\n        config\n            .subscriptions\n            .into_iter()\n            .filter(|s| s.active)\n            .map(|s| {\n                let usage_percentage = if let Some(limit) = s.monthly_tokens {\n                    if limit > 0 {\n                        (s.current_usage as f64 / limit as f64) * 100.0\n                    } else {\n                        0.0\n                    }\n                } else {\n                    0.0\n                };\n\n                let status = if usage_percentage >= 100.0 {\n                    \"depleted\"\n                } else if usage_percentage >= 90.0 {\n                    \"max_out\"\n                } else if usage_percentage < 50.0 {\n                    \"accelerate\"\n                } else {\n                    \"on_pace\"\n                };\n\n                let reset_time = if let Some(ref renewal) = s.renewal_date {\n                    renewal.clone()\n                } else {\n                    \"monthly\".to_string()\n                };\n\n                SubscriptionInfo {\n                    name: s.provider,\n                    quota_used: s.current_usage,\n                    quota_limit: s.monthly_tokens,\n                    reset_time,\n                    status: status.to_string(),\n                }\n            })\n            .collect()\n    }\n\n    /// Read recent events from audit log.\n    async fn read_recent_events(&self) -> Vec<EventInfo> {\n        let content = match tokio::fs::read_to_string(&self.audit_log_path).await {\n            Ok(c) => c,\n            Err(e) => {\n                debug!(\"Failed to read audit log: {}\", e);\n                return vec![];\n            }\n        };\n\n        // Parse JSONL and get last 10 entries\n        let mut events = Vec::new();\n        for line in content.lines().rev().take(10) {\n            if let Ok(entry) = serde_json::from_str::<AuditLogEntry>(line) {\n                events.push(EventInfo {\n                    timestamp: entry.timestamp,\n                    event_type: description: entry.command,\n                    worker: None,\n                    bead_id: None,\n                });\n            }\n        }\n\n        events\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for RealContextSource {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait::async_trait]\nimpl ContextSource for RealContextSource {\n    async fn gather(&self) -> Result<DashboardContext> {\n        // Gather all data concurrently\n        let (workers, tasks, (costs_today, costs_projected), subscriptions, recent_events) = tokio::join!(\n   tasks_completed: Option<i64>,\n}\n\n/// Bead JSON format from br CLI.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize)]\nstruct BeadJson {\n    id: String,\n    title: String,\n    priority: u8,\n    #[serde(default)]\n    assignee: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Subscriptions config format.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize)]\nstruct SubscriptionsConfig {\n    subscriptions:
